item.title: The first American autonomous ground vehicles are fighting in Ukraine
item.dc:creator: Tim Fernholz
item.description: Forterra has deployed more than 100

item.title: Netflix invented binge-watching. Now it may have outgrown it.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Perez
item.description: A new report suggests Netflix viewers aren’t sticking around for Season 2. The bigger issue may be that binge-watching itself is no longer the advantage it once was.

item.title: The ‘first’ AI-run ransomware attack still needed a human
item.dc:creator: Connie Loizos
item.description: An AI agent carried out the technical execution of a real-world ransomware attack for the first known time, but new details show a human still chose the victim, set up the infrastructure, and supplied stolen credentials — meaning it wasn't quite the fully autonomous cybercrime debut that last week's headlines suggested.

item.title: US investors will soon get access to SK Hynix, another memory maker riding the AI boom
item.dc:creator: Julie Bort
item.description: SK Hynix is experiencing a boom credited to AI. It will ride that to a multibillion-dollar U.S. IPO, expected to take place on Friday.

item.title: Vercel CEO Guillermo Rauch on the fight to split off models from agents
item.dc:creator: Russell Brandom
item.description: "The reality is, when you're optimizing for production, you start looking at a price/performance," Guillermo Rauch tells TechCrunch.

item.title: You can now customize Siri’s pace and expressivity in the latest iOS 27 beta
item.dc:creator: Sarah Perez
item.description: The update is part of Apple's broader effort to make Siri feel more natural and personal, as it rebuilds the assistant around generative AI.

item.title: Every major tech layoff in 2026 that has name-checked AI
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Bellan, Connie Loizos
item.description: A running look — in reverse chronological order — at the bigger tech companies that have announced significant layoffs this year with AI as a stated factor.

item.title: Amazon competitor Bookshop.org says Kobo eReader support will happen this year after all
item.dc:creator: Julie Bort
item.description: Bookship.org seemed to delay this anticipated partnership again, but tells TechCrunch that it has settled business terms and is working on integration.

item.title: If you use Google, you’re training its AI. Here’s how to opt out.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Perez
item.description: Consider this a belated PSA: A recent change to Google’s privacy settings is allowing the company to store more of your data, including media such as “images, files, and audio and video recordings,” to improve its AI models.

item.title: Apple brings back card payments for Apple Account purchases in India after a four-year hiatus
item.dc:creator: Jagmeet Singh
item.description: Apple has started a phased rollout of card payments for Apple Account purchases in India after adapting to the country's payments framework.

item.title: Microsoft lays off nearly 5,000 employees across Xbox, commercial sales
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Bellan
item.description: Microsoft cut around 4,800 roles, or 2.1% of its global workforce, on Monday — the latest in a series of layoffs that’s stoking fears of AI replacing jobs. The layoffs will hit Xbox and commercial sales the hardest.

item.title: Reddit is using LLMs to solve a problem LLMs largely created
item.dc:creator: Amanda Silberling
item.description: In the AI era, platforms have no choice but to fight fire with fire to cull spam.

item.title: Canadian spy agency says it hacked drug traffickers, extremists, and a ransomware gang last year
item.dc:creator: Zack Whittaker
item.description: The hacking operations disclosed in a Canadian spy agency's annual report underscores some pressing national security threats facing the country and its top allies.

item.title: How to reserve and change your WhatsApp username
item.dc:creator: Ivan Mehta
item.description: WhatsApp now lets users reserve usernames ahead of the feature’s full rollout, making it possible to connect without sharing a phone number once usernames go live.

item.title: Station F ramps up as a launchpad for Europe’s hottest AI startups
item.dc:creator: Anna Heim
item.description: Station F, a Paris-based startup hub founded by French billionaire Xavier Niel, is gearing up for a new edition of its F/ai accelerator program in a bid to strengthen its positioning as a stepping stone for promising AI startups.

item.title: Smart glasses maker Even Realities hits $1B valuation with $150M funding led by Meituan, Tencent
item.dc:creator: Kate Park
item.description: Even Realities, an ex-Apple team building camera-free smart glasses, raised $150 million from Meituan and Tencent at a $1 billion valuation.

item.title: This humanoid robotics company is going public, but its CEO isn’t promising a robot in your home anytime soon
item.dc:creator: Connie Loizos
item.description: While other humanoid startups chase sky-high valuations, Agility Robotics is betting its future on execution — and a SPAC.

item.title: Uber’s European expansion plans may have hit a speed bump
item.dc:creator: Anthony Ha
item.description: Back in February, Uber announced ambitious plans to launch in seven new European markets in 2026 — but now five of those launches are reportedly on hold.

item.title: Trump memecoin investors lost $3.8 billion, analysis finds
item.dc:creator: Anthony Ha
item.description: Nearly 1 million people have lost a total of $3.8 billion after buying President Donald Trump’s $TRUMP memecoin, while Trump made $636 million.

item.title: Amazon will stop accepting new customers for Mechanical Turk
item.dc:creator: Anthony Ha
item.description: These may be the last days of Amazon’s Mechanical Turk.
